articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Italy held to scoreless draw by Turkey in Euro 2024 warmup
article.description: Italy and Turkey played out a disappointing 0-0 draw in a game of few chances on Tuesday, in what was the penultimate warmup game for both sides before Euro 2024 starts in 10 days.
article.linkText: Italy, Turkey end scoreless in Euro 2024 warmup

images.name: Italy v Turkey friendly [1296x729]
images.caption: Italy's Jorginho and Turkey's Abdulkerim Bardakci in action during the international friendly between Italy and Turkey, in Bologna, Italy, on June 4.

peers.name: Italy v Turkey friendly [1296x729]
peers.caption: Italy's Jorginho and Turkey's Abdulkerim Bardakci in action during the international friendly between Italy and Turkey, in Bologna, Italy, on June 4.

article.story:
Italy and Turkey played out a disappointing 0-0 draw in a game of few chances Tuesday, in what was the penultimate warmup for both sides before Euro 2024 starts in 10 days.

Luciano Spalletti's Italy go into the tournament as defending champions, and after failing to impress while qualifying, this draw with Turkey will do little to boost the confidence of their manager or fans.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, NWSL more (U.S.)

We weren't sharp or consistent, but in some moments the team pushed high and tried to do things, it's the classic game at the start of the process," Spalletti said.

"Maybe, I expected a bit more quality in the opponent's half, without that quality everything becomes more difficult."

Spalletti was without midfielder Nicolò Barella, out with a minor muscle strain, while striker Gianluca Scamacca also sat this one out having only arrived at training on Monday, with Mateo Retegui starting up front.

The opening half ended with neither side registering a shot on target, although Italy's Bryan Cristante rattled the post with a header from a corner-kick on the stroke of half-time.

"You have to be able to change quickly, the team suffered a bit of fatigue, up front we weren't brilliant, we tried to do something different but by then the game was over," Spalletti said.

Turkey manager Vincenzo Montella will be concerned over the condition of defender Ozan Kabak who was taken off injured with what looked like a serious knee issue late in the first half.

Spalletti will name his official 26-man squad on Thursday, after playing a training match with the Under-20 side on Wednesday, but this performance, while extending their unbeaten run to five games, will bring more questions than answers.

"It takes a while to get in place. There's still tomorrow's game to make choices, we take all the time we can," Spalletti said.

"I'm satisfied because the game was real, intense, you go and get to know certain things."

Turkey, after topping their qualification group with just one defeat, will be satisfied enough with the draw, against a side they have never beaten, having slumped to two losses in their March friendlies, including a 6-1 thumping by Austria.

Italy's final warm-up game before heading to Germany is against Bosnia on Sunday while Turkey travel to face Poland on Monday.

At Euro 2024, Italy will face Spain, Croatia and Albania in Group B, and Turkey take on Georgia, Portugal and Czech Republic in Group F.
